decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.3°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective first step is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip, leveraging the primary recommended variable within safe bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity by 1.5x enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the local hot spot intensity on the chip.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation study.
